description: A dish made by cooking beaten eggs in a pan, either folded plain or filled with ingredients such as cheese, herbs, vegetables, or meat.
preparation_steps: Heat butter or oil in a pan.
Cook the eggs gently until they begin to set.
Fold and serve when the omelette reaches the desired texture.

health_advice.benefits: Eggs provide protein and the dish can be nutrient-dense when vegetables are included.
health_advice.risks: Extra butter, cheese, and processed meat can raise saturated fat and sodium.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Add vegetables and avoid overloading the omelette with heavy fillings.

dietary_notes.note: A plain or vegetable omelette is vegetarian. It is naturally gluten-free unless fillings or sauces introduce gluten.

allergens: Possible dairy if butter or cheese is used
common_variations: Spanish omelette style preparations differ and are usually thicker
